title: jarava filifolia
intro: Jarava filifolia, also known as Queñua or Q'ente, is a plant species native to the Andean region, belonging to the grass family (Poaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Jarava filifolia include relief from coughs, colds, and fever, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues and wounds. In traditional medicine, Jarava filifolia is often prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the dried leaves and flowers in hot water.
